meaning: n.洞,窟窿,山谷adj.空的,虚伪的,空腹的,凹的adv.<口>彻底vi.形成空洞vt.挖空,弄凹
examples: The tree trunk was hollow inside.
The squirrel disappeared into a hollow at the base of the tree.
His proud boast of leading "the party of low taxation" has a hollow ring.
The disadvantage is that capsules are easier (and therefore cheaper) to make than hollow, fluid-filled fibres.
The argument that Parliament has become ineffectual must have a hollow ring to government ministers.
